core_mechanism: a weight-tied (parameter-shared) deep compositional nonlinear map that recursively fuses streams sampled at different rates onto a common resolution — replacing a fixed linear projection with a stagewise-shared learned operator whose depth grows with the number of inputs — accompanied by a universal-approximation result and a non-asymptotic error bound
brief_reason: The abstract replaces linear frequency alignment with a per-frequency deep network using parameter sharing across stages to allow deeper networks for more high-frequency inputs, and proves an approximation theory plus a non-asymptotic prediction-error bound for this shared compositional map.
